---
title: "Chapter 9 - Raja-Vidya Raja-Guhya Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">राजविद्याराजगुह्ययोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-08.html">Previous chapter</a><a href="chapter-10.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-9-1" data-reference="9.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-1">9.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">To you, who do not cavil, I shall declare this most secret knowledge together with its realization; knowing it, you will be freed from ill.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">इदं तु ते गुह्यतमं प्रवक्ष्याम्यनसूयवे ।<br>
ज्ञानं विज्ञानसहितं यज्ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसेऽशुभात् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-2" data-reference="9.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-2">9.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">This is the royal knowledge, the royal secret, the supreme purifier, plain to direct experience, in keeping with <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span>, easy to practice, and imperishable.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">राजविद्या राजगुह्यं पवित्रमिदमुत्तमम् ।<br>
प्रत्यक्षावगमं धर्म्यं सुसुखं कर्तुमव्ययम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-3" data-reference="9.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-3">9.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">People who put no faith in this <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span>, O scorcher of foes, fail to reach me and return to the path of death and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="samsara: The ongoing cycle of worldly becoming, birth, death, and attachment." aria-label="samsara: The ongoing cycle of worldly becoming, birth, death, and attachment." title="samsara: The ongoing cycle of worldly becoming, birth, death, and attachment." data-glossary-id="samsara">samsara</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अश्रद्दधानाः पुरुषा धर्मस्यास्य परन्तप ।<br>
अप्राप्य मां निवर्तन्ते मृत्युसंसारवर्त्मनि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-4" data-reference="9.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-4">9.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">By me, in my unmanifest form, this whole world is pervaded; all beings dwell in me, but I do not dwell in them.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मया ततमिदं सर्वं जगदव्यक्तमूर्तिना ।<br>
मत्स्थानि सर्वभूतानि न चाहं तेष्ववस्थितः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-5" data-reference="9.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-5">9.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">And yet beings do not dwell in me—behold my sovereign <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>! Sustaining beings, yet not dwelling in them, my self is what brings beings into being.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न च मत्स्थानि भूतानि पश्य मे योगमैश्वरम् ।<br>
भूतभृन्न च भूतस्थो ममात्मा भूतभावनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-6" data-reference="9.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-6">9.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">As the vast wind, moving everywhere, abides always in space, so, understand, all beings abide in me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यथाकाशस्थितो नित्यं वायुः सर्वत्रगो महान् ।<br>
तथा सर्वाणि भूतानि मत्स्थानीत्युपधारय ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-7" data-reference="9.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-7">9.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">All beings, O son of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." aria-label="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." title="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." data-glossary-id="kaunteya">Kunti</span>, pass into my <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." aria-label="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." title="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." data-glossary-id="prakriti">prakriti</span> at the end of an aeon, and at the aeon&#x27;s beginning I send them forth again.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्वभूतानि कौन्तेय प्रकृतिं यान्ति मामिकाम् ।<br>
कल्पक्षये पुनस्तानि कल्पादौ विसृजाम्यहम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-8" data-reference="9.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-8">9.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Resting on my own <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." aria-label="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." title="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." data-glossary-id="prakriti">prakriti</span>, I send forth again and again this whole host of beings, helpless under the sway of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." aria-label="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." title="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." data-glossary-id="prakriti">prakriti</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रकृतिं स्वामवष्टभ्य विसृजामि पुनः पुनः ।<br>
भूतग्राममिमं कृत्स्नमवशं प्रकृतेर्वशात् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-9" data-reference="9.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-9">9.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Nor do these actions bind me, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." aria-label="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." title="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." data-glossary-id="dhananjaya">Dhananjaya</span>; I remain seated apart, unattached to those actions.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न च मां तानि कर्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनञ्जय ।<br>
उदासीनवदासीनमसक्तं तेषु कर्मसु ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-10" data-reference="9.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-10">9.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Under my oversight, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." aria-label="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." title="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." data-glossary-id="prakriti">prakriti</span> gives birth to all that moves and does not move;<br>
by this cause, O son of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." aria-label="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." title="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." data-glossary-id="kaunteya">Kunti</span>, the world turns round.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मयाध्यक्षेण प्रकृतिः सूयते सचराचरम् ।<br>
हेतुनानेन कौन्तेय जगद्विपरिवर्तते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-11" data-reference="9.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-11">9.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The foolish scorn me when I have taken on a human form, not knowing my higher being as the great lord of beings.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अवजानन्ति मां मूढा मानुषीं तनुमाश्रितम् ।<br>
परं भावमजानन्तो मम भूतमहेश्वरम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-12" data-reference="9.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-12">9.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Their hopes vain, their actions vain, their knowledge vain, bereft of sense, they fall into a deluding, fiendish, and brutish nature.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मोघाशा मोघकर्माणो मोघज्ञाना विचेतसः ।<br>
राक्षसीमासुरीं चैव प्रकृतिं मोहिनीं श्रिताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-13" data-reference="9.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-13">9.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But the great souls, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, sheltering in the divine nature, worship me with undivided mind, knowing me as the imperishable source of beings.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">महात्मानस्तु मां पार्थ दैवीं प्रकृतिमाश्रिताः ।<br>
भजन्त्यनन्यमनसो ज्ञात्वा भूतादिमव्ययम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-14" data-reference="9.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-14">9.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Ever glorifying me, striving with firm vows, bowing before me with devotion, they worship me, ever steadfast.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सततं कीर्तयन्तो मां यतन्तश्च दृढव्रताः ।<br>
नमस्यन्तश्च मां भक्त्या नित्ययुक्ता उपासते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-15" data-reference="9.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-15">9.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Others, offering the sacrifice of knowledge, worship me as the one, as the many, and as facing in all directions, in my manifold forms.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ज्ञानयज्ञेन चाप्यन्ये यजन्तो मामुपासते ।<br>
एकत्वेन पृथक्त्वेन बहुधा विश्वतोमुखम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-16" data-reference="9.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-16">9.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I am the rite, I am the sacrifice, I am the offering to the ancestors, I am the herb;<br>
I am the sacred formula, I am the melted butter, I am the fire, I am the oblation.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अहं क्रतुरहं यज्ञः स्वधाहमहमौषधम् ।<br>
मन्त्रोऽहमहमेवाज्यमहमग्निरहं हुतम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-17" data-reference="9.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-17">9.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I am the father of this world, its mother, sustainer, and forefather; the object of knowledge, the purifier, the syllable <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." aria-label="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." title="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." data-glossary-id="om">Om</span>, and the Rig, the Sama, and the Yajur <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Veda</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पिताहमस्य जगतो माता धाता पितामहः ।<br>
वेद्यं पवित्रमोङ्कार ऋक्साम यजुरेव च ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-18" data-reference="9.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-18">9.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I am the goal, the upholder, the lord, the witness, the dwelling, the refuge, the friend; the origin, the dissolution, the ground, the resting place, the imperishable seed.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">गतिर्भर्ता प्रभुः साक्षी निवासः शरणं सुहृत् ।<br>
प्रभवः प्रलयः स्थानं निधानं बीजमव्ययम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-19" data-reference="9.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-19">9.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I give heat; I hold back the rain and send it forth; I am immortality and death, being and nonbeing, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तपाम्यहमहं वर्षं निगृह्णाम्युत्सृजामि च ।<br>
अमृतं चैव मृत्युश्च सदसच्चाहमर्जुन ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-20" data-reference="9.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-20">9.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The knowers of the three <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Vedas</span>, who drink the soma and are cleansed of sin, worship me with sacrifices and seek the way to heaven; reaching the holy world of Indra, they taste in heaven the divine pleasures of the gods.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">त्रैविद्या मां सोमपाः पूतपापा<br>
यज्ञैरिष्ट्वा स्वर्गतिं प्रार्थयन्ते ।<br>
ते पुण्यमासाद्य सुरेन्द्रलोक-<br>
मश्नन्ति दिव्यान्दिवि देवभोगान् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-21" data-reference="9.21">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-21">9.21</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Having enjoyed that vast world of heaven, their merit spent, they enter the world of mortals; so, following the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span> of the three <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Vedas</span> and craving pleasures, they come and go.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ते तं भुक्त्वा स्वर्गलोकं विशालं<br>
क्षीणे पुण्ये मर्त्यलोकं विशन्ति ।<br>
एवं त्रयीधर्ममनुप्रपन्ना<br>
गतागतं कामकामा लभन्ते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-22" data-reference="9.22">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-22">9.22</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But those who, thinking of no other, worship me, ever yoked to me—for them I secure what they lack and preserve what they have.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते ।<br>
तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-23" data-reference="9.23">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-23">9.23</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Even those devoted to other gods who worship them with faith, O son of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." aria-label="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." title="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." data-glossary-id="kaunteya">Kunti</span>, worship me too, though not in the appointed way.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">येऽप्यन्यदेवता भक्ता यजन्ते श्रद्धयान्विताः ।<br>
तेऽपि मामेव कौन्तेय यजन्त्यविधिपूर्वकम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-24" data-reference="9.24">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-24">9.24</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For I am the enjoyer and the lord of all sacrifices; but they do not know me in truth, and so they fall.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अहं हि सर्वयज्ञानां भोक्ता च प्रभुरेव च ।<br>
न तु मामभिजानन्ति तत्त्वेनातश्च्यवन्ति ते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-25" data-reference="9.25">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-25">9.25</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Those who worship the gods go to the gods; those who worship the ancestors go to the ancestors; those who worship the spirits go to the spirits; but those who worship me come to me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यान्ति देवव्रता देवान्पितॄन्यान्ति पितृव्रताः ।<br>
भूतानि यान्ति भूतेज्या यान्ति मद्याजिनोऽपि माम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-26" data-reference="9.26">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-26">9.26</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Whoever offers me with devotion a leaf, a flower, a fruit, or water—that offering of devotion, given by the pure of heart, I accept.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पत्रं पुष्पं फलं तोयं यो मे भक्त्या प्रयच्छति ।<br>
तदहं भक्त्युपहृतमश्नामि प्रयतात्मनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-27" data-reference="9.27">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-27">9.27</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer or give, whatever austerity you practice, O son of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." aria-label="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." title="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." data-glossary-id="kaunteya">Kunti</span>, do it as an offering to me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यत्करोषि यदश्नासि यज्जुहोषि ददासि यत् ।<br>
यत्तपस्यसि कौन्तेय तत्कुरुष्व मदर्पणम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-28" data-reference="9.28">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-28">9.28</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">So you will be freed from the bonds of action, from its good and evil fruits;<br>
your self yoked in the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> of renunciation, set free, you will come to me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">शुभाशुभफलैरेवं मोक्ष्यसे कर्मबन्धनैः ।<br>
संन्यासयोगयुक्तात्मा विमुक्तो मामुपैष्यसि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-29" data-reference="9.29">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-29">9.29</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I am the same toward all beings; none is hateful to me and none dear;<br>
but those who worship me with devotion are in me, and I too am in them.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">समोऽहं सर्वभूतेषु न मे द्वेष्योऽस्ति न प्रियः ।<br>
ये भजन्ति तु मां भक्त्या मयि ते तेषु चाप्यहम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-30" data-reference="9.30">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-30">9.30</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Even one of very evil conduct who worships me, devoted to none else, is to be reckoned among the good, for his resolve is right.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अपि चेत्सुदुराचारो भजते मामनन्यभाक् ।<br>
साधुरेव स मन्तव्यः सम्यग्व्यवसितो हि सः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-31" data-reference="9.31">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-31">9.31</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Swiftly he becomes righteous of soul and attains lasting peace;<br>
know it for certain, O son of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." aria-label="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." title="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." data-glossary-id="kaunteya">Kunti</span>—no one devoted to me is lost.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">क्षिप्रं भवति धर्मात्मा शश्वच्छान्तिं निगच्छति ।<br>
कौन्तेय प्रतिजानीहि न मे भक्तः प्रणश्यति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-32" data-reference="9.32">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-32">9.32</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For those who take refuge in me, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, even if born of a lowly womb—women, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="vaishya: Member of the class associated with trade, agriculture, and herding." aria-label="vaishya: Member of the class associated with trade, agriculture, and herding." title="vaishya: Member of the class associated with trade, agriculture, and herding." data-glossary-id="vaishya">vaishyas</span>, and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="shudra: Member of the service class in the traditional varna scheme." aria-label="shudra: Member of the service class in the traditional varna scheme." title="shudra: Member of the service class in the traditional varna scheme." data-glossary-id="shudra">shudras</span>—even they reach the supreme goal.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मां हि पार्थ व्यपाश्रित्य येऽपि स्युः पापयोनयः ।<br>
स्त्रियो वैश्यास्तथा शूद्रास्तेऽपि यान्ति परां गतिम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-33" data-reference="9.33">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-33">9.33</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">How much more, then, the pure brahmins and devoted royal sages! Having come to this fleeting, joyless world, worship me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">किं पुनर्ब्राह्मणाः पुण्या भक्ता राजर्षयस्तथा ।<br>
अनित्यमसुखं लोकमिमं प्राप्य भजस्व माम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-9-34" data-reference="9.34">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-9-34">9.34</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Fix your mind on me, be devoted to me, sacrifice to me, bow before me; so, yoking yourself, with me as your highest goal, you will come to me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु ।<br>
मामेवैष्यसि युक्त्वैवमात्मानं मत्परायणः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
